patch.value: Create/copy ID allocation TOCTOU: duplicate generated id silently overwrites the other item's file; idExists misses extension type folders

Two gaps in new-item id allocation under parallel creates. (1) TOCTOU + silent overwrite: generateItemId (src/core/item/id.ts) does a check-then-use existence probe long before writeCreatedItem acquires the per-id lock (create.ts runs parent/blocked-by resolution I/O in between), and the final writeFileAtomic RENAMES OVER any existing file. If two parallel creates draw the same id (4-char base36 = 1.68M space; a 20-create parallel burst has ~1/9000 pair-collision odds, worse as the workspace fills and token space is consumed), the second create silently destroys the first item — no error, no history anomaly on the surviving file. Fix: after acquiring the per-id lock in writeCreatedItem/copy, re-check id existence and regenerate (or fail with id_collision) before writing; or write the new item with O_EXCL semantics (open wx) instead of overwrite-rename. (2) idExists only probes built-in TYPE_TO_FOLDER folders (.md/.toon), not extension-registered item-type folders, so a generated id can collide with an extension-typed item undetected — thread the resolved type_to_folder registry into generateItemId. Empirical baseline: 30 parallel creates → 30 items, 0 collisions observed (expected at this scale); this is an extreme-case correctness guard, not an observed failure.
